revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24699703697|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Fudaten-jinja]][[ja:布多天神社]] {{Expand Japanese|布多天神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Fudaten Shrine | native_name = 布多天神社 | image = Fudaten-Shrine-02.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Fudaten Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.656912|139.545814}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|ca|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|fr|Sukunabikona|it|Sukuna-hikona|ja|スクナビコナ|ko|스쿠나비코나|mni|ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯕꯤꯀꯣꯅ|pl|Sukunabikona|pt|Sukunahikona|simple|Sukunabikona|sv|Suku-Na-Bikona|zh|少彥名|qid=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}} }} {{nihongo|'''Fudaten Shrine'''|布多天神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} * {{ill|Tenmangū|lt=Tenmangū (worship)|da|Temmangu|en|Tenmangū|es|Tenman-gū|fr|Sanctuaire tenman-gū|ja|天満宮|ko|덴만구|simple|Tenjin shrines|zh|天滿宮|qid=Q798838|1=Tenmangū}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11546334|ltq=Q11546334|qid=Q11546334|1=List of Shikinaisha in Musashi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 6 ** P155: administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q210667|ltq=Q210667|qid=Q210667|1=Chōfu}} * {{ill|Q907398|ltq=Q907398|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.656912|139.545814}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Fudaten-Shrine-02.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|ca|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|fr|Sukunabikona|it|Sukuna-hikona|ja|スクナビコナ|ko|스쿠나비코나|mni|ꯁꯨꯀꯨꯅꯕꯤꯀꯣꯅ|pl|Sukunabikona|pt|Sukunahikona|simple|Sukunabikona|sv|Suku-Na-Bikona|zh|少彥名|qid=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}} University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8012405000987 == Commons category (P373) == * Fudaten-jinja == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '東京都調布市調布ケ丘1丁目8-1[1]', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Fudaten-jinja}} [[JA:布多天神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11480773}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Fudaten Shrine |画像 =[[File:Fudaten-Shrine-02.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}{{ill|lt=Chōfu|Q210667|qid=Q210667|1=Chōfu}}{{ill|lt=Chōfugaoka|Q49005780|qid=Q49005780|1=Chōfugaoka}} 1-8-1{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Office}} |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|39|24.2|N|139|32|43.9|E|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|1=Sukunabikona}}
{{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Office}} |社格 = {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}（small）
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = (trad.) reign of the 11th emperor {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|qid=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}} |本殿 ={{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 =Goshuku Tenjin{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Office}} |例祭 =September 25{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Office}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Western Tokyo|lat_deg=35|lat_min=39|lat_sec=24.2|lon_deg=139|lon_min=32|lon_sec=43.9|label=Fudaten Shrine|position=left|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|39|24.2|N|139|32|43.9|E}}}} }} '''Fudaten Shrine''' (ふだてんじんしゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Chōfugaoka|Q49005780|qid=Q49005780|1=Chōfugaoka}}, {{ill|lt=Chōfu|Q210667|qid=Q210667|1=Chōfu}}, {{ill|lt=Tokyo|Q1490|qid=Q1490|1=Tokyo}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is the {{ill|lt=guardian shrine|Q10324625|qid=Q10324625|1=Manuel de Barros}} of {{ill|lt=Fuda Goshuku|Q28690549|qid=Q28690549|1=Fuda Goshuku}} and is also called “Goshuku Tenjin”{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Office}}. === Enshrined deities === Originally {{ill|lt=Sukunabikona|Q3275687|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|1=Sukunabikona}} was the deity, and at the time of relocation {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} was {{ill|lt=merged|Q11412524|qid=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}. Because the name Fudaten Shrine appears in the Engishiki, it is one of the shrines called “{{ill|lt=Tenjin-sha|Q11443279|qid=Q11443279|1=Tenjinsha}}” from before enshrining Sugawara no Michizane. There are various examples of shrines with Sukunabikona-no-Kami as main deity being called Tenjin-sha. === Foundation === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says it was founded about 1,940 years ago in the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Suinin|Q314850|qid=Q314850|1=Emperor Suinin}}. It is an ancient shikinaisha listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} (compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}) under Tama District, Musashi Province{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki, Kami-Fuda-juku}}. The original shrine stood in today’s Fuda 5-chōme. After major flooding of the {{ill|lt=Tama River|Q1063006|qid=Q1063006|1=Tama River}} in the {{ill|lt=Bunmei|Q1193544|qid=Q1193544|1=Bunmei}} era, it moved to the current location in Bunmei 9 ({{ill|lt=1477|Q6614|qid=Q6614|1=1477}}) and merged Sugawara no Michizane{{sfn|Tokyo Shrine Office}}. A “Taikō proclamation board” from April {{ill|lt=1590|Q6764|qid=Q6764|1=1590}} ({{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 18), issued by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|qid=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} during his Odawara campaign to reassure people, remains in shrine holdings. The former shrine site now has “Kotenjin Park.” Excavation in Shōwa 55 found traces from about 10,000 years ago ({{ill|lt=Paleolithic|Q40203|qid=Q40203|1=Paleolithic}}), settlement traces from about 4,000-5,000 years ago ({{ill|lt=Jōmon period|Q52824|qid=Q52824|1=Jōmon period}}), and tombs from about 1,500 years ago. Villages of Fuda Goshuku (Fuda-Kojima-bun, Kami-Ishihara, Shimo-Ishihara, Kami-Fuda, Shimo-Fuda, and Kokuryō-juku) merged with Tobitagyū and Kamigakyū in 1889 (Meiji 22) to form {{ill|lt=Chōfu Town|Q11631758|qid=Q11631758|1=Chōfu}}, making this shrine the general guardian shrine of Chōfu Town. === Precincts === File:Fudaten-jinja, honden.jpg|Honden enclosure File:Fudaten-jinja, kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall File:Fudaten-jinja, komainu-1.jpg|Komainu (A, Chōfu City designated cultural property) File:Fudaten-jinja, komainu-2.jpg|Komainu (Un, Chōfu City designated cultural property) File:Fudaten-Shrine-01.jpg|Torii === Cultural properties === === Chōfu City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Fudaten Shrine honden (with one ridge tag) (building) - designated December 25, 1998. * Tangible folk cultural properties ** Fudaten Shrine komainu pair - designated November 12, 1969. ** Fudaten Shrine Taikō proclamation board - designated September 8, 1971. === Antique market === An {{ill|lt=antique market|Q11668600|qid=Q11668600|1=antique market}} is held on the second Sunday of every month, with around 20-30 stalls[https://tsugesanpo.sakura.ne.jp/18.html Walking with Tsuge Yoshiharu (Chōfu edition) official site, 18. Fudaten Shrine]. === Setting in works === === In works by Shigeru Mizuki === In {{ill|lt=Shigeru Mizuki|Q669049|qid=Q669049|1=Shigeru Mizuki}}’s ''{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hakaba Kitarō|1=UNKNOWN}}'' and ''{{ill|lt=GeGeGe no Kitarō|Q1368693|qid=Q1368693|1=GeGeGe no Kitarō}}'', Kitarō is set as living in the forest behind the honden. Mizuki, who lived in {{ill|lt=Chōfu|Q210667|qid=Q210667|1=Chōfu}}, is said to have used the forest of Fudaten Shrine, connected to Taishōji cemetery, as inspiration. In season 6 of the TV anime, following the original work and set in Chōfu, there is a scene where Kitarō returns to the back of the precinct{{Cite web|和書|title=Visit locations of the anime "GeGeGe no Kitarō"! Pilgrimage map distribution starts in Chōfu, Tokyo|url=https://animetourism88.com/ja/news/1474|website=Anime Tourism Association - 88 Sacred Sites|accessdate=2019-02-14|language=ja}}. The approach from {{ill|lt=Old Kōshū Kaidō|Q11511097|qid=Q11511097|1=Old Kōshū Kaidō}} is lined with shops as “Tenjin-dōri Shopping Street,” and as a revitalization project, statues of yōkai characters from ''GeGeGe no Kitarō'' are installed. === Other === *In Tsuge Yoshiharu’s ''{{ill|lt=The Man Without Talent|Q3203779|qid=Q3203779|1=The Man Without Talent}}'' series episode “Selling the Camera,” the temple-fair antique market is depicted. The last scene with Sukegawa and his child heading quietly toward the {{ill|lt=torii|Q234731|qid=Q234731|1=Torii}} after leaving Fudaten Shrine is memorable. *Used as a location in the 1997 TV drama ''{{ill|lt=Aoi Tori (TV drama)|Q11661048|qid=Q11661048|lt=Aoi Tori|1=Aoi Tori}}'', depicted as a shrine in {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|qid=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}. *Used as a location in the 2019 TV drama ''{{ill|lt=Anata no Ban Desu|Q61058243|qid=Q61058243|1=Your Turn to Kill}}''. *Used as a location in the 2023 TV drama ''{{ill|lt=VIVANT|Q116919744|qid=Q116919744|1=Vivant (TV series)}}'' as a contact point for the protagonists’ special unit. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/kitatama/chofu/3087/|title= Fudaten Shrine [Goshuku Tenjin]|publisher=Tokyo Shrine Office|accessdate=2020-7-26|ref=harv}} * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|qid=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}|volume=Vol. 93 Tama District No.5|chapter=Kami-Fuda-juku Fudaten Shrine|id={{NDLJP|763989/17}}|publisher=Geography Bureau, Home Ministry|date=1884-6|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki, Kami-Fuda-juku}}}} === Related literature === * {{cite book|和書|title={{ill|lt=Edo Meisho Zue|Q2704637|qid=Q2704637|1=Edo meisho zue}}|id={{NDLJP|1174144/154}}|publisher=Yūhōdō Shoten|editor=Saitō Chōshū|volume=2|series=Yūhōdō Bunko|year=1927|chapter=Vol. 3 Tenki section '''Fudaten Shrine'''|pages=298-299}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Rengeiji (Chōfu)|Q43594264|qid=Q43594264|1=Rengeiji (Chōfu)}} * {{ill|lt=Fuda Goshuku|Q28690549|qid=Q28690549|1=Fuda Goshuku}} * {{ill|lt=Daima Shrine|Q24861920|qid=Q24861920|1=Daima Shrine}} - shrine inside {{ill|lt=Kadokawa Daiei Studio|Q11630733|qid=Q11630733|1=Kadokawa Daiei Studio}}. === External links === {{Commonscat|Fudaten-jinja}} * [http://www.fudatenjin.or.jp Fudaten Shrine] - official site {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=東京都}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Fudaten Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Fudaten Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Tenmangū (worship)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:調布市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chōfu, Tokyo]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Tenjin (kami)]]
